beers: Where the Wild Things Are - Girraween (2025)
Where the Wild Things Are - Maleny (2025)

beer_details.beer_description: Troubled in its youth, Pilsner has returned to its roots, hops. A modern expression, with the classic. peppy finish. All the NZ heros are there, Motueka, Riwaka, Peacherine, Waimea and Manilita. Late kettle, WP and a DDH with some extra Peacharine HopKief for the finishing zhuzh. Fruit forward, with classic tropical, grapefruit, stonefruit and guava characters. The dry body brings drinkability, with a white pepper finish, leaving you wanting another.

We’re rolling up at the lights next to our mates from @hikerbrewing, and by "rolling up" we mean nervously stalling it twice in a clapped-out four-banger.

This cracking West Coast DIPA is full beans straight out the tap, hops flat to the floor, heel-toeing between bitterness and malt sweetness in a pair of Dan's not-so-metaphorical thongs.

It’s full throttle ballet in a glass - Simcoe, Mosaic and Motueka bouncing off the rim like a tacho kissing the limiter, with a clutch dump full of pine, resin, lime citrus and finishing with a tropical skid on your tastebuds. It'll hit harder than a mis-shift into second and get your palate rattling like the gearbox in our delivery van.

Quadrupling down to get the most thiol loaded hazy we can manage, we’ve partnered with the boffins at Freestyle Hops to go full beer geek. Kicking off with Undermodified Pilsner malt from Voyager. With less modification comes more bound thiols, the precursors to thiol flavour. Next comes Sauvignon Blanc juice. Rich in 3MH (grapefruit), 3MHA (passionfruit) and 4MMP (gooseberry). Up comes Nelson Sauvin and Mega Motueka in the mash. At this temperature the mash proteases unlock the 4MMP and 3S4MP from the Nelson and Mega Motueka, which is a red wine musk infused version of Motueka. Extremely rich in thiols. The Manilita and Waimea add rich NZ hop aromatics. Fermentation sees a strong dry hop hit at high krausen, where the yeast activity brings biotransformation with alacrity. Here, more Mega Mot, Nelson and Manilita shine, releasing thiols in abundance. A cold dry hop adds to the overall intensity and brings those unique NZ aromatic to the fore. 

It’s a big old complex juice bomb. Unmistakably kiwi with an overload of passionfruit, blackcurrant, grapefruit, gooseberry and diesel. Super charged aromatics over a soft and pillowy body. Land of the long white cloud indeed, it’s soft and gentle, with the hop flavours on show. A joy to develop something that really pushes a hop element as far as it can go, with our mates at Freestyle.

Inspired by the classics cocktail, this lime juice kettle sour was infused with juniper berries and orange zest. The lactose adds balance to the citric sourness, with fresh lime juice dominating. Juniper berries were macerated in neutral spirit for 7 days, before being infused with some orange zest and dosed into the final product.

Lime and botanicals on the nose, with a lime cordial and red berry mid palate and salty sour finish. All the flavours in balance, leading to a refreshing experience tailored to these long summer arvos.

It’s the breakfast cereal beer you never knew you wanted. Lemon, lime and orange over a corn syrup body and citrus sour finish. A generous, fun filled pack of breakfast sour beer.

All the citrus comes first. Candied versions of lemon, lime and orange giving Saturday morning cartoons, with pjs all day. Some corn like viscous body brings the cereal character, with more generic citrus flavours through the middle. The finish is both sour and sweet, to deliver drinkability. Nostalgic yet novel.

This one lives past the redline. Built on instinct and pushed without restraint, we locked in maximum juice and tuned it for a bright, dangerously drinkable ride. Mosaic lays down the power, while Peacharine and Waimea hit the NOS and send it screaming. Familiar names, zero complacency - a calculated gamble at full throttle, where the risk is real and the reward is everything.

Tropical, stone and citrus fruits crash at high speed in a juiced up flavour bomb. Peacharine and Waimea delivers classic stone fruit, with hints of guava and diesel. Mosaic delivers that familiar citric-tropical party. The rolled oats and wheat keep the body full but drinkable.

Even the most fastidious plan comes unravelled when Working Title gets involved. A super smooth and silky RIS, aimed at Makers Mark Bourbon Barrels for a short sojourn, left for 12 months because we laugh in the face of scheduling. The result is unexpected but superior, like it was always meant to be.

12 malts drive the chocolate complexity; with savoury biscuit, sweet caramel and bitter chocolate all contributing their opinion. Gentle notes of rye, oats and wheat add to the cacophony. A perfect RIS combo of Willamette, Simcoe and Columbus barely waylay the chaos of dank pine, minty spice and a balanced white pepper bitterness. An ambitious project that confused itself in its simplicity.

Because the journey is more important than its destination, each year we finish with a beer that champions our discovery of hop things in the inevitable Pursuit of Happiness. Four things we learned in 2025; Mash hopping drives biotransformation, Simcoe is the perfect Red IPA hop, more hops at more times is more and Riwaka Amplifier from Clayton Hops is a powerful ally.

2025 saw us return to our favourite IPA style: Red. Mucking about with terpenes, thiols and esters is all well and good, but the confluence of rich malts with rich hops is what floats our boats. This year we focused on hopping all over the gaff, from mash in to first wort, coolpools, day 0, high krausen, chill back and cold DH. The results were clear: adding more hops at more times points gives richer hop experience. Intuitive, for sure, but you never never know if you never never jam them in everywhere. Some hoppings didn’t work for the Reds, in our opinion. Day 0, late ferment and too much in high krausen were all either just not impactful or too astringent. Overall, the strategy for hopping Red IPAs is congruent with the malt bill, just add more!

2013; craft beer had arrived and Brisbane was ready. Real beer, made with proper ingredients, no corners cut and focused on flavour. It was a bold move to land on an aggressively hopped, crystal and biscuit malt loaded American IPA but the reception was positive. Quickly it become the flagship, a totem of the movement, fresh IPA.

Many years and many iterations later, we've gone back to the original recipe. Cascade, Citra, Simcoe and Centennial. With a Carahell and Biscuit malt backbone, the structure is supportive of big hop additions. Bitterness is high, but may not be perceived from the viscous body. Pine, resin, and grapefruit mostly, with some subtle pineapple and stone fruit hits along the way. Big, bold and uncompromising, a journey that's come full circle.

beer_details.beer_name: Where the Wild Things Are - Girraween (2025)
beer_details.beer_description:
Last time we looked at Dan’s wild captured yeast in a 12 month barrel ferment. This time, we’ve gone fully stainless ferment, to allow the subtle yeast characters to really be the hero. A two week primary ferment in this context, allows the yeast to shine without influences from the oak or aging process.

This insanely unique yeast profile really presents like an Australian Saison, with predominant esters of peach, pear and red apple, layered with some stone fruits, and with some very soft peppery phenolics for balance. It drinks full, even though it’s incredibly dry. Familiar but distinct, what a great expression of Australian Wild Beer.

beer_details.beer_name: Where the Wild Things Are - Maleny (2025)
beer_details.beer_description:
A spontaneously harvested, native mixed culture 3 years in the making. Hot wort made in Doggett St was raced up to the family farm and laid down in mini koelships in various different plots, directly exposed to the elements. Allowed to cool over night, samples were collected the next morning and fermented for a few weeks in demijohns. Selecting samples that saw a good fermentation profile and had attractive aromatic attributes, they were scaled up into carbouys. A further round of culling saw a final scale for the best sample up to neutral oak. A 12 month, slow ferment allowed the full expression of the mixed culture. From this 300L batch, a further 12 month scale up was made to 5 x 300L barrels, allowing us to pack this QLD first, and get it into your hands.

Subtle, bread-like, stone fruit and floral esters on the nose, provide a complex array of unique aromatics. Brett and sour bacteria like character is also evident. A tart, zippy finish leaves the overall impression full but refreshing. A dominant sour exotic stone fruit character pervades the experience. Maleny never tasted so funky.

A six month barrel fermented Flanders inspired Golden Sour was blended with young Belgian Blond, and refermented on fresh peach juice. Seeking that balance of a true Flanders, where fruit driven sweetness combines with puckering acetic driven acidity.

Delicate peach aromatics combine with a Brettanomyces bruxellensis funk and Acetobacter sourness. A gentle honey treacle mid palate softness the complex sourness, providing a balanced drinkability.

Dark meets light in this dark lager. A smooth and sessionable dark lager with notes of roasted nuts, bread crusts and caramel. With a light body, it comes without the heaviness that usually comes with darker beer styles. This is the perfect warm-weather thirst quencher. Light body, full taste. Keg only.
Easy drinking and refreshing Aussie Summer Ale. Brewed in a dedicated Gluten Free Brewery.
Grouse Amber Ale - Gluten Free

Our IPA is bursting with flavour and bite, giving off pine and fruity aromatics. A beautifully-hopped IPA brewed with millet and buckwheat; showcasing Galaxy and Mosaic hops. Gold medal winner at both the INDIES and AIBAs. It's also endorsed by Coeliac Australia!

YEAST: US05
MALTS: Pale Millet Malt, Vienna Millet Malt, Goldfinch Millet Malt, Cara Millet Malt
HOPS: Galaxy, Mosaic, Amarillo, Magnum

Toriaezu, biiru! (First and foremost, a beer!). Rice has been a staple ingredient in Japanese brewing for millennia, giving beers a lighter colour, taste and body, making them refreshing with low bitterness. Lightly hopped, this ultra dry, super crisp lager is an easy drinker that will keep you coming back for another glass. Kanpai!

Full size serve, full strength, but without the carbs.

Japanese Rice Lager is lower in carbohydrate and lower in bitterness. This easy drinking, full strength beer is a perfect thirst quencher for the carb conscious beer lover. 

YEAST: 34/70
MALTS: Malted Millet, Malted Rice, Raw Rice
HOPS: Saphir, Mosaic, Incognito

A clean, fruitful lager, showcasing Vienna Millet Malt, brewed as an easy-drinking, light pale straw-coloured version of a German Pilsner. TWØBAYS fourth core beer features tangerine and citrus aromas, with a smooth, crisp palate, hints of delicate grains and a sharp refreshing finish.

Corn chips and dips have a best friend, and it’s called Cerveza! And just like corn chips, there’s a good dose of corn in this brew too. This Mexican style lager is cool, crisp and easy drinking - if it were in a bottle, you’d have a wedge of lime in it for sure.

The World's Best GF Beer - World Beer Cup Gold 2025

New to the core range, Pacific Ale has dethroned our very own Session Ale as the World's best GF beer at the World Beer Cup 2025. It is packed with Australian and NZ hops. Low bitterness with a big, fruity hop profile makes this an easy drinking and sessionable beer. Expect hoppy notes of pine, pineapple and citrus.

If you are looking for a flavourful beer experience at a sessionable ABV and without the gluten, this might be the perfect beer for you. All TWØBAYS core range beers are endorsed by Coeliac Australia.
Easy drinking American hopped ale with fruity palate and citrus aromas

Raspberry Berliner Weisse features a powerful raspberry aroma, backed up by refreshing tart and juicy flavours and a dry finish. Brewed at a lower 3.5% abv, this is the perfect warm-weather thirst quencher
A Mosaic-hopped IPA with a hazy finish, featuring tropical fruit flavours and a crisp, lean palate.
Hazy IPA
Berliner Visa aged in a whisky barrel with the addition of blood orange juice. Tart, bold and juicy, this bloody Berliner will refresh the palate and is perfect as a ready-made blood orange mimosa
Conehead IPA is for all the hop lovers out there that love their IPA's dank and bitter, resinous and gripping, aromatic and punchy. We'd like to think there's a little Conehead in all of us.

Feijoas are part of the Myrtle family and their character is one that will surely divide. The combined with the the natural funk and acid from the bacteria and yeast as well as 100g/L of feijoa fruit make for a truely delicious wild ale.

Our Italian Wild Grape Ale makes its third release, this time with beer from our more than 100 year old foeder... The base beer compliments the Savagnin grape varietal from our good mates at Crittenden Estate Winery on the Mornington Peninsula.  We dosed a higher percentage of grape marc into the base beer so the grape flavour is wonderfully bold and works so well with the wild nature of our foeder beer.  Sherbert/Champagne like effervescence and a light body from the grape juice makes this dangerously drinkable and it would work perfectly in a Champagne flute!

Inspired by the ales of Northern England, we have crafted a Red Ale that walks the fine line of drinkability and robust flavour. 
Burnt caramel and biscuit dominate the palate with subtle fruity and herbal esters. Despite its rough edges, it's a brew with undeniable charm, perfect for those who appreciate a bit of grit with their pint.

Tastes Boss.
A great beer is all about balance & drinkability. With a malt bill that provides balance to the hop selection, our pale ale is truly pint-worthy. Light citrus hop character amplifies the refreshment.

Boatrocker Pilsner is a New World Pilsner. It's our little nod to the amazing pilsners from Northern Germany...with a little love from New Zealand hops. We crafted this beer with only the best German pilsner malt, then loaded it up with some choice Kiwi hops (including late boil hop bursting & dry hopping).
Jam packed with tropical fruitflayva, this is like drinking a punch bowl with all your favourites- passionfruit, mango, pineapple and more! Tart and tropical, punch bowl is the ultimate refreshment!
Starward Whisky Barrel aged

Aged in Starward Projects co-ferment casks. An Australian First by Starward. American oak imparts lush soft vanillins with toffee, blackberry & quince notes from the spirit, working wonderfully well with the dark malts
